candidate_problems.text: Can the conjecture be made non-circular by specifying a precise mechanism by which self-referential record-using systems generate temporal orientation, rather than merely presupposing the past/future asymmetry already contained in records, memory, and persistence? The key open problem is whether 'record' and 'self-successor' can be defined in a fundamentally time-symmetric way, and then shown to break symmetry only at the level of loop organization.

What is the dependence relation between the thermodynamic arrow and the proposed epistemic/loop-structural arrow: does self-referential modeling genuinely add a new explanatory layer, or is it entirely downstream of low-entropy boundary conditions and record formation? This matters because if the loop arrow cannot diverge from or constrain the thermodynamic arrow, the conjecture may be a redescription of standard statistical mechanics rather than a new account.

What empirical or formal discriminators would distinguish a world with time-symmetric laws plus self-referential strange loops from one where temporal direction is fully explained without self-reference? In particular, can the conjecture predict when non-conscious record-keeping systems, artificial agents, or minimally self-modeling organisms should exhibit an intrinsic arrow, and when they should not?
